title: Filter a List by Class — cfilter
text: list containing elements `x` () inherit specified class.

title: Filter a List by Class — cfilter
code: lst <- list(1L, "text", 3.14, list(a = 1)) cfilter(lst, "numeric")  # returns elements that are numeric #> [[1]] #> [1] 3.14 #>  cfilter(lst, "character")  # returns character elements #> [[1]] #> [1] "text" #>  cfilter(lst, "numeric", not = TRUE)  # returns elements that are NOT numeric #> [[1]] #> [1] 1 #>  #> [[2]] #> [1] "text" #>  #> [[3]] #> [[3]]$a #> [1] 1 #>  #>

title: Create a YAML-style list from a named vector or list — create_yml
code:
# Create a simple YAML list from a named vector x <- c(name = "Alice", age = 30, country = NA) create_yml(x) #> [1] "- name: Alice
  age: 30
  country: 
  "

title: Check lme4 Model Convergence Status — lme4_has_converged
text: integer indicating convergence status: 1 Converged successfully (convergence issues). 0 Model singular (random effects may redundant). -1 Convergence issues detected, model singular.

title: Check lme4 Model Convergence Status — lme4_has_converged
text: function inspects `@optinfo$conv$lme4` slot model object determine optimizer reported convergence problems. also checks singularity using `isSingular()`. function copied Robert Long https://stackoverflow.com//72128391/9032257.

title: Check lme4 Model Convergence Status — lme4_has_converged
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(lme4) fm <- lmer(Reaction ~ Days + (Days | Subject), sleepstudy) lme4_has_converged(fm) } # }

what: Campionamento Casuale Semplice o Stratificato — sample_by_group
title: Campionamento Casuale Semplice o Stratificato — sample_by_group
text: Questa funzione permette di estrarre un campione casuale di righe da un data frame. È possibile eseguire un campionamento globale oppure un campionamento stratificato raggruppando dati per una o più colonne specificate.

title: Campionamento Casuale Semplice o Stratificato — sample_by_group
Campionamento Casuale Semplice o Stratificato — sample_by_group
text: data Un data.frame da cui estrarre il campione. n Un intero positivo che indica il numero di righe da estrarre per ogni gruppo. Se il gruppo ha meno di n righe, viene restituito l'intero gruppo. Predefinito 1. Un vettore di caratteri (es. "colonna_A" o c("col1", "col2")) che specifica le colonne di raggruppamento. Se NULL (predefinito), il campionamento avviene sull'intero dataset.

title: Campionamento Casuale Semplice o Stratificato — sample_by_group
text: Un data.frame contenente le righe campionate, con nomi delle righe (rownames) resettati.

title: Campionamento Casuale Semplice o Stratificato — sample_by_group
text: La funzione utilizza internamente split e lapply per gestire sottogruppi. Se un gruppo risultante è vuoto, non contribuirà al risultato finale.

title: Campionamento Casuale Semplice o Stratificato — sample_by_group
code: # Campionamento di 2 righe dal dataset iris (globale) sample_by_group(iris, n = 2) #>   Sepal.Length Sepal.Width Petal.Length Petal.Width    Species #> 1          5.1         2.5          3.0         1.1 versicolor #> 2          5.4         3.7          1.5         0.2     setosa  # Campionamento di 1 riga per ogni Specie in iris sample_by_group(iris, n = 1, by = "Species") #>   Sepal.Length Sepal.Width Petal.Length Petal.Width    Species #> 1          5.1         3.5          1.4         0.2     setosa #> 2          6.6         2.9          4.6         1.3 versicolor #> 3          6.2         3.4          5.4         2.3  virginica  # Campionamento stratificato su più colonne (se applicabile) # sample_by_group(mtcars, n = 2, by = c("cyl", "am"))

what: Calculate Standard Error and Confidence Interval for a Proportion — se_p
title: Calculate Standard Error and Confidence Interval for a Proportion — se_p
text: function computes standard error asymptotic (Wald) confidence interval given proportion based specified number simulations observations.

title: Calculate Standard Error and Confidence Interval for a Proportion — se_p
Calculate Standard Error and Confidence Interval for a Proportion — se_p
text: p numeric value representing proportion (0 1). nsim integer representing number simulations sample size. conf.level numeric value 0 1 specifying confidence level interval. Defaults 0.95.

title: Calculate Standard Error and Confidence Interval for a Proportion — se_p
text: list containing: se: calculated standard error proportion. ci: named numeric vector lower bound (ci.lb)     upper bound (ci.ub) confidence interval.

title: Calculate Standard Error and Confidence Interval for a Proportion — se_p
text: standard error calculated : $$SE = \sqrt{\frac{p(1-p)}{n}}$$ confidence interval calculated using standard normal distribution (Z-score) approximation.

title: Calculate Standard Error and Confidence Interval for a Proportion — se_p

title: Filter knitr output by line indices — set_filter_output
Filter knitr output by line indices — set_filter_output
text: Invisibly returns NULL. Called side effects.

title: Filter knitr output by line indices — set_filter_output
text: function modifies knitr's global output hook. Call (e.g., setup chunk) activate behavior rest document. hook: splits output lines using xfun::split_lines() selects lines via internal helper filor:::.grep_lines() passes filtered output original knitr output hook

title: Filter knitr output by line indices — set_filter_output
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ set_filter_output()  ## In an R Markdown chunk: ## ```{r, lines = c(1, 3, 5)} ## print(1:10) ## ``` } # }

what: Highlight Selected Lines of Printed Output as HTML — style_output
title: Highlight Selected Lines of Printed Output as HTML — style_output
text: Captures printed output R object, escapes HTML, highlights selected lines using either inline background color CSS class. result wrapped `<pre><code>` tags printed output.

title: Highlight Selected Lines of Printed Output as HTML — style_output
Highlight Selected Lines of Printed Output as HTML — style_output
text: x R object whose printed representation captured. lines Integer vector indicating lines output highlighted. Passed .grep_lines() selection. hg Character string specifying background color use highlighting class NULL. Defaults "yellow". class Optional character string giving CSS class name apply highlighted lines. provided, hg ignored.

title: Highlight Selected Lines of Printed Output as HTML — style_output
text: function called side effect. prints HTML code output returns invisible(NULL).

title: Highlight Selected Lines of Printed Output as HTML — style_output
text: function uses capture.output obtain printed representation x htmlEscape ensure HTML safety. Line selection delegated internal helper .grep_lines().

title: Highlight Selected Lines of Printed Output as HTML — style_output
code:
x <- summary(lm(mpg ~ wt, data = mtcars)) style_output(x, lines = 2:3) #> 
 #> Call: #> lm(formula = mpg ~ wt, data = mtcars) #>  #> Residuals: #>     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max  #> -4.5432 -2.3647 -0.1252  1.4096  6.8727  #>  #> Coefficients: #>             Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)     #> (Intercept)  37.2851     1.8776  19.858  < 2e-16 *** #> wt           -5.3445     0.5591  -9.559 1.29e-10 *** #> --- #> Signif. codes:  0 ‘***’ 0.001 ‘**’ 0.01 ‘*’ 0.05 ‘.’ 0.1 ‘ ’ 1 #>  #> Residual standard error: 3.046 on 30 degrees of freedom #> Multiple R-squared:  0.7528,	Adjusted R-squared:  0.7446  #> F-statistic: 91.38 on 1 and 30 DF,  p-value: 1.294e-10 #>  #>
# Using a CSS class instead of inline color style_output(x, lines = 1, class = "highlight") #> 
 #> Call: #> lm(formula = mpg ~ wt, data = mtcars) #>  #> Residuals: #>     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max  #> -4.5432 -2.3647 -0.1252  1.4096  6.8727  #>  #> Coefficients: #>             Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)     #> (Intercept)  37.2851     1.8776  19.858  < 2e-16 *** #> wt           -5.3445     0.5591  -9.559 1.29e-10 *** #> --- #> Signif. codes:  0 ‘***’ 0.001 ‘**’ 0.01 ‘*’ 0.05 ‘.’ 0.1 ‘ ’ 1 #>  #> Residual standard error: 3.046 on 30 degrees of freedom #> Multiple R-squared:  0.7528,	Adjusted R-squared:  0.7446  #> F-statistic: 91.38 on 1 and 30 DF,  p-value: 1.294e-10 #>  #>

what: Summarize an object with optional extra computations — summary_mcs
title: Summarize an object with optional extra computations — summary_mcs
text: Applies summary function object optionally attaches additional computations attribute.

title: Summarize an object with optional extra computations — summary_mcs
code: summary_mcs(x, summary = NULL, summary.args = NULL, extra = NULL)

title: Summarize an object with optional extra computations — summary_mcs
text: x object summarized. summary function used summarize x. NULL, x returned unchanged. summary.args list additional arguments passed summary via .call(). extra optional list functions. function applied original x, results stored "mcs" attribute output.

title: Summarize an object with optional extra computations — summary_mcs
text: summary provided, returns result summary(x, ...); otherwise returns x. extra supplied, returned object additional attribute "mcs" containing list extra results.

title: Summarize an object with optional extra computations — summary_mcs
text: Functions supplied extra always evaluated original input x, summarized output.

title: Summarize an object with optional extra computations — summary_mcs
code: summary_mcs(   x = rnorm(10),   summary = summary,   extra = list(     mean = mean,     sd = sd   ) ) #>    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max.  #> -1.9796 -0.0532  0.3841  0.1747  0.9528  1.3940

what: Pairwise name combinations (as concatenated strings) — wcor
title: Pairwise name combinations (as concatenated strings) — wcor
text: Generate 2-combinations set names return pair single concatenated string (e.g., `"ab"`, `"ac"`, ...).

title: Pairwise name combinations (as concatenated strings) — wcor
Pairwise name combinations (as concatenated strings) — wcor
text: n Integer. Number elements (number names generate `names` `NULL`). names Character vector length `n`. `NULL`, defaults `letters[1:n]`.

title: Pairwise name combinations (as concatenated strings) — wcor
text: character vector length `choose(n, 2)` containing concatenated   name pairs.

title: Pairwise name combinations (as concatenated strings) — wcor
text: `names` provided, first `n` lowercase letters (`letters[1:n]`) used default names. Uses [gtools::combinations()] generate unordered pairs `names`, concatenates pair separator.

title: Pairwise name combinations (as concatenated strings) — wcor
code: wcor(4) #> Error in loadNamespace(x): there is no package called ‘gtools’ wcor(3, c("x", "y", "z")) #> Error in loadNamespace(x): there is no package called ‘gtools’
